To:
public.engagement@uscis.dhs.gov
publicengagementfeedback@uscis.dhs.gov
reportfraudtips@uscis.dhs.gov

Subject: Consideration of Hong Kong Police Chief Superintendent TSE Chun Chung John’s application to migrate to the US

Dear Sir/Madam

The movement for freedom and democracy in Hong Kong enters its fourth month in October. As the momentum of the movement grows, so has police brutality. While police officers rain violence and terror on Hong Kong people, their actions are defended and endorsed by their superiors, one of them is Mr Tse Chun Chung, John (Chief Superintendent)(CSP Tse), head of the Police Public Relations Branch of the Hong Kong Police Force. I understand CSP Tse is applying to emigrate to the United States of America and would like to point out his role in the Hong Kong movement for your consideration when processing his application.

CSP Tse heads the daily police press conference which sums up the actions taken previously by the Police Force. Not only has he failed to address public concern and anger about escalating police brutality when enforcing the law, but even rationalised and justified his colleagues’ reckless and aggressive behaviour. Three examples are given below to illustrate my point.

1. Attacks on unarmed civilians

During the protests in Tsuen Wan on the night of 25 August, an unarmed man knelt in front of police line, hands raised, and begged officers not to shoot the protestors behind him. He was kicked away by an officer without prior warning, who had already drawn his gun.

At the regular press conference on the next day (26 August), CSP Tse whitewashed the brute act as a ‘push’ and described it as a reflex action. He further commended the officer for taking a necessary and reasonable action at the split of a second.

2. Rising aggression against reporters

Sporadic assaults on members of the press like kicking, pepper-spraying or teargassing were recorded since the movement erupted in June. However, such attacks have exacerbated recently.
On 29 September, an Indonesian journalist (Ms Veby Mega Indah)was shot by the police in the eye in Wan Chai and local reporters were hit by rubber bullets while covering demonstrations. Ms Veby’s right eye is permanently blind as a result of the injury.

At the following police press conference, CSP Tse said that police were dispersing the large amount of protestors on a footbridge and that the beanbag rounds fired were not aimed at reporters. But news clips show that protestors were scarce on the footbridge in question moments before the shooting and were in retreat. Standing on the same footbridge was Ms Veby, wearing a fluorescent vest and helmet, both with the word ‘PRESS’ printed clearly on them. She was shot on the footbridge by retreating riot police moments after she began Facebook live stream. CSP Tse lied to cover up the vengeful acts of his colleagues even when video records tell a completely different story.

3. Teenager shot by live ammunition

On 1 Oct, China’s national day, massive protests erupted across the territory to rally against the increasing curtailment of freedom in Hong Kong. Live ammunition was first used.

During the clashes in Tsuen Wan district, an 18-year old school boy was shot point-blank in the chest. X-ray showed that the bullet was but 3cm from his heart. News footage showed that the boy was wielding a white PVC pipe at armed riot police. Out of the array of weapons (baton and pepper spray) at his disposal, the officer fired his gun at the teenager. Riot police then crowded around him, delaying medical treatment for some three minutes and planted a sharpened iron pole on the injured. The medical and education sectors condemned the shooting as ‘execution’.

CSP Tse dismissed the widespread condemnation at the reckless shooting, saying that non-disciplinary organisations had no basis and lacked firearms knowledge to criticise or question the professionalism of trained police officers.

He further drew audiences’ attention to the sharpened poles, hammers and wrenches protestors were armed with, which posed serious threat to riot police.

What CSP Tse omitted was that the Police Force relaxed the guidelines on using weapons the night before so that if officers merely perceived that their lives were ‘likely’ to be under threat, they could use lethal weapons like live ammunition.

CSP Tse sides with and defends a Force that brutally crush people fighting for basic human rights - civil liberties, democracy, rights to free speech and assembly. He repeatedly lies for the Hong Kong Police Force, a Force of violence and vengeance. Is he fit to be a citizen of the free world, the United States of America? Does he appear to share the core values of the free men and women in the US? I urge the US government to consider his application to migrate into the free world with prudence.

Yours sincerely
A Hong Kong citizen

********************

香港人，反抗

//其實777知佢仲未可以動用到緊急法，但佢特登令我地自己營造緊急打仗狀態，到司法覆核既時候佢既律師就可以同法庭講 “係呀之前都可能未justified 但依家就唔同啦”

星期五第一個司法覆核就係咁輸

岩岩今日入court 個份係本世紀最強既律師團一齊整，真係寫得好好，但如果我地又自己搞到打仗咁就完全幫緊林太。

如果呢個覆核成功，成條緊急法都會被廢除，個盒唔會再有人有得玩。//

今日休息下，俾和理非/大律師做野，我地絕不割蓆不篤灰，今日係做國際文宣，比外媒影相，今日唔裝唔放火。最尾衰左，前線先出手！

高院受理左司法覆核，一定要同律師同一陣線，唔好俾機會政府！

Subject: Consideration of Hong Kong Police Chief Superintendent TSE Chun Chung John’s application to migrate to the US

Dear Sir/Madam

The movement for freedom and democracy in Hong Kong enters its fourth month in October. As the momentum of the movement grows, so has police brutality. While police officers rain violence and terror on Hong Kong people, their actions are defended and endorsed by their superiors, one of them is Mr Tse Chun Chung, John (Chief Superintendent)(CSP Tse), head of the Police Public Relations Branch of the Hong Kong Police Force. I understand CSP Tse is applying to emigrate to the United States of America and would like to point out his role in the Hong Kong movement for your consideration when processing his application.

CSP Tse heads the daily police press conference which sums up the actions taken previously by the Police Force. Not only has he failed to address public concern and anger about escalating police brutality when enforcing the law, but even rationalised and justified his colleagues’ reckless and aggressive behaviour. Three examples are given below to illustrate my point.

1. Attacks on unarmed civilians

During the protests in Tsuen Wan on the night of 25 August, an unarmed man knelt in front of police line, hands raised, and begged officers not to shoot the protestors behind him. He was kicked away by an officer without prior warning, who had already drawn his gun.

At the regular press conference on the next day (26 August), CSP Tse whitewashed the brute act as a ‘push’ and described it as a reflex action. He further commended the officer for taking a necessary and reasonable action at the split of a second.

quote.msg:
3. Teenager shot by live ammunition

On 1 Oct, China’s national day, massive protests erupted across the territory to rally against the increasing curtailment of freedom in Hong Kong. Live ammunition was first used.

During the clashes in Tsuen Wan district, an 18-year old school boy was shot point-blank in the chest. X-ray showed that the bullet was but 3cm from his heart. News footage showed that the boy was wielding a white PVC pipe at armed riot police. Out of the array of weapons (baton and pepper spray) at his disposal, the officer fired his gun at the teenager. Riot police then crowded around him, delaying medical treatment for some three minutes and planted a sharpened iron pole on the injured. The medical and education sectors condemned the shooting as ‘execution’.

CSP Tse dismissed the widespread condemnation at the reckless shooting, saying that non-disciplinary organisations had no basis and lacked firearms knowledge to criticise or question the professionalism of trained police officers.

He further drew audiences’ attention to the sharpened poles, hammers and wrenches protestors were armed with, which posed serious threat to riot police.

What CSP Tse omitted was that the Police Force relaxed the guidelines on using weapons the night before so that if officers merely perceived that their lives were ‘likely’ to be under threat, they could use lethal weapons like live ammunition.

CSP Tse sides with and defends a Force that brutally crush people fighting for basic human rights - civil liberties, democracy, rights to free speech and assembly. He repeatedly lies for the Hong Kong Police Force, a Force of violence and vengeance. Is he fit to be a citizen of the free world, the United States of America? Does he appear to share the core values of the free men and women in the US? I urge the US government to consider his application to migrate into the free world with prudence.

Yours sincerely
A Hong Kong citizen

********************

香港人，反抗

quote.msg:
2. Rising aggression against reporters

Sporadic assaults on members of the press like kicking, pepper-spraying or teargassing were recorded since the movement erupted in June. However, such attacks have exacerbated recently.
On 29 September, an Indonesian journalist (Ms Veby Mega Indah)was shot by the police in the eye in Wan Chai and local reporters were hit by rubber bullets while covering demonstrations. Ms Veby’s right eye is permanently blind as a result of the injury.

At the following police press conference, CSP Tse said that police were dispersing the large amount of protestors on a footbridge and that the beanbag rounds fired were not aimed at reporters. But news clips show that protestors were scarce on the footbridge in question moments before the shooting and were in retreat. Standing on the same footbridge was Ms Veby, wearing a fluorescent vest and helmet, both with the word ‘PRESS’ printed clearly on them. She was shot on the footbridge by retreating riot police moments after she began Facebook live stream. CSP Tse lied to cover up the vengeful acts of his colleagues even when video records tell a completely different story.

文宣新方法！ 以連豬做四格漫畫！！
https://lih.kg/1635818
- 分享自 LIHKG 討論區

見到south park 咁大回響，就發現文宣最緊要易入口，好似 Joan Cornella咁，將每人發生既事記錄落黎，大家睇幾秒就明曬，而且又容易翻譯做唔同語言。而且漫畫容易引人追睇，易入屋又易收藏

已經係手足可以幫手畫畫，如果連登仔一齊諗橋，其實一日兩三篇絕對唔難，又可以支援國際戰線，又可以吸引多d人睇！

大家可以幫手諗埋角色設定：

連豬： 深黃勇武派

連狗： 和理非

腦魔： 記者？

其實一開始可以畫d易入口d, 好似老夫子咁深入淺出容易吸引d深黃淺藍 （深黃跟本唔洗睇佢都明），又容易translate做其他語言，例如：

1.為所欲為Tee ( 講腦魔教連狗著為所欲為tee，然後比連豬整蠱

2. 連豬病都冇得帶口罩，變咗豬流感

3.或者用連豬連狗將一d好人好事，黃絲小店分享比大家，例如全民幫黃店清潔同say sorry（或者講下咩店唔幫襯得）

大家幫手推！
建議d examples加埋YouTube link 上去
